learn.title: India's Green Hydrogen Push 🌿
learn.intro: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels. This initiative aligns with global climate goals and positions India as a leader in clean energy. By leveraging renewable energy for hydrogen production, the mission supports decarbonization in industries like steel and cement, which are major emitters.
learn.c1_text: The mission targets 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen production by 2030, requiring 125 GW of renewable energy capacity. This is equivalent to powering millions of homes annually. A key goal is to cut India's carbon footprint by replacing coal-based hydrogen with renewable-powered alternatives.
learn.c2_text: The mission offers financial incentives, including production-linked subsidies and tax benefits, to manufacturers. For example, companies can receive up to ₹15,000 per tonne of hydrogen produced. This reduces costs and encourages private investment in scaling up production facilities.
learn.c3_text: Green hydrogen is critical for heavy industries in India, such as steel manufacturing, which accounts for 7% of the country's emissions. Pilots in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat demonstrate how hydrogen can replace fossil fuels in furnaces, cutting emissions by up to 30% per unit of output.
learn.stat1: India aims for 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen production by 2030.
learn.stat2: The mission requires 125 GW of renewable energy capacity to support hydrogen production.
learn.stat3: Hydrogen production could reduce India's coal imports by 6 million tonnes annually.
learn.stat4: Initial investments in hydrogen infrastructure could reach ₹1.5 lakh crores by 2030.

learn.title: Solar Manufacturing Incentives 🌞
learn.intro: India's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme boosts domestic solar panel manufacturing, making solar energy more affordable. By incentivizing local production, the policy reduces import dependency and creates jobs. This is crucial for achieving India's solar capacity target of 500 GW by 2030.
learn.c1_text: The PLI scheme provides ₹20,000 crores in incentives over five years. Manufacturers receive subsidies based on the output of solar modules produced domestically. For instance, a company producing 1 GW of panels could earn ₹2,000 crores in incentives.
learn.c2_text: The scheme aims to increase domestic manufacturing share to 60% by 2026. This reduces costs for consumers, as locally made panels are cheaper. For example, solar panel prices have dropped by 20% since the PLI's launch in 2021.
learn.c3_text: The PLI has already generated over 10,000 jobs in solar manufacturing. States like Gujarat and Tamil Nadu are hubs, with companies like Adani and Tata expanding plants. This supports India's goal of self-reliance in renewable technology.
learn.stat1: ₹20,000 crores in incentives allocated over five years.
learn.stat2: Domestic solar module manufacturing share target: 60% by 2026.
learn.stat3: Solar panel prices dropped 20% since PLI launch.
learn.stat4: Over 10,000 jobs created in solar manufacturing.

learn.title: Solar as Building Design 🏗️
learn.intro: Building Integrated Photovoltaics (BIPV) embeds solar panels into building materials like roofs and windows. This approach turns structures into energy generators, reducing land use for solar farms. BIPV is gaining traction in India for urban sustainability and aesthetic integration.
learn.c1_text: BIPV replaces traditional building materials with solar panels. For example, solar tiles on rooftops generate electricity while serving as coverings. A 1,000 sq ft building with BIPV can produce 5-10 kWh daily, enough to power basic appliances.
learn.c2_text: In cities like Bengaluru and Mumbai, BIPV is used in commercial buildings and housing complexes. A hotel in Jaipur uses BIPV glass for its lobby, cutting energy costs by 30% annually. This is ideal for dense areas where land is scarce.
learn.c3_text: India's BIPV market is projected to grow at 25% CAGR. The government supports BIPV through the Solar Mission, offering subsidies for integration. A 2023 study showed 15% of new commercial buildings in Delhi use BIPV, saving ₹50 lakh annually in energy bills.
learn.stat1: BIPV market growth: 25% CAGR in India.
learn.stat2: 15% of new commercial buildings in Delhi use BIPV.
learn.stat3: A Jaipur hotel saved ₹50 lakh annually with BIPV.
learn.stat4: BIPV can reduce land use for solar by 40% in urban areas.

learn.title: ISTS Waiver Policy 🌐
learn.intro: India's Inter-State Transmission System (ISTS) waiver policy reduces costs for renewable energy projects by exempting them from transmission charges. This policy has lowered the levelized cost of solar and wind power by 15-20%, making renewables more competitive and accelerating grid integration.
learn.c1_text: ISTS is the network that carries electricity across states. Normally, projects pay 10-15% of their revenue as transmission fees. The waiver removes this cost, allowing projects in remote areas (like Rajasthan's solar farms) to sell power at lower prices.
learn.c2_text: The waiver has cut project costs by an average of ₹2-3 lakh per MW. For a 100 MW solar plant, this saves ₹2-3 crores annually. This makes renewables cheaper than diesel-based power in many regions, boosting adoption.
learn.c3_text: In 2022, the waiver helped 20 GW of renewable projects avoid ₹4,000 crores in transmission costs. States like Gujarat and Maharashtra benefit most, as they host large solar and wind farms. This policy is key to India's 500 GW renewable target by 2030.
learn.stat2: ₹4,000 crores saved in 2022 for 20 GW of projects.
learn.stat3: Waiver covers 70% of inter-state transmission costs.
learn.stat4: 20 GW of renewable projects benefited in 2022.

learn.title: Solar Inverters Explained ⚡
learn.intro: Solar inverters convert DC electricity from panels into AC power for homes. Choosing between string, micro, or hybrid inverters affects efficiency and cost. Understanding these types helps optimize solar systems, especially in India's diverse climate and grid requirements.
learn.c1_text: String inverters connect multiple panels in a series. They are cost-effective but less efficient if one panel is shaded. For example, a 5 kW system with a string inverter costs ₹1-1.5 lakh and works best for unshaded rooftops.
learn.c2_text: Micro inverters are attached to each panel, optimizing individual performance. A 5 kW system with micro inverters costs ₹2-2.5 lakh but handles shading better. This is ideal for Indian homes with partial shade from trees or structures.
learn.c3_text: Hybrid inverters manage both solar and battery storage. A 5 kW system with a hybrid inverter costs ₹3-3.5 lakh. It allows storing excess solar energy for nighttime use, critical in India where grid outages are common.
learn.stat1: String inverters cost ₹1-1.5 lakh for 5 kW systems.
learn.stat2: Micro inverters cost ₹2-2.5 lakh for 5 kW systems.
learn.stat3: Hybrid inverters cost ₹3-3.5 lakh for 5 kW systems.
learn.stat4: Hybrid inverters enable 24/7 solar usage with storage.

learn.title: EV Grid Stability 🚗⚡
learn.intro: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology lets electric vehicles (EVs) supply power back to the grid during peak demand. This stabilizes the grid, reduces strain, and turns EVs into mobile energy storage. In India, V2G could enhance grid resilience while supporting EV adoption.
learn.c1_text: V2G uses bidirectional chargers to draw power from an EV's battery to the grid. For example, a 60 kWh EV battery can supply 6 kW for an hour. This is managed via smart software that coordinates with the grid's needs.
learn.c2_text: V2G can reduce peak demand by 10-15% during heatwaves. In California, V2G pilots saved $2 million in grid costs. In India, this could prevent blackouts during monsoon seasons when demand spikes.
learn.c3_text: India's 20 million EV target by 2030 creates V2G opportunities. A pilot in Bengaluru showed a 20 kWh EV could provide 2 kW for 10 hours, supporting 20 homes. With incentives, V2G could add 5 GW of flexible storage by 2030.
learn.stat1: V2G can reduce peak demand by 10-15%.
learn.stat2: California saved $2 million via V2G pilots.
learn.stat4: Bengaluru pilot: 20 kWh EV supports 20 homes for 10 hours.
